metadata.description: The network address-assignment and name-resolution SERVICE for a site — DHCP and/or DNS as one operated capability (two projections of the same allocation: a lease and its A/PTR records). Vendor mechanisms (Kea, dnsmasq, BIND, FreeIPA, UniFi, a cloud service) are providers, never the type. It is the service surface a DHCP/DNS provider realizes, rendering leases/reservations from the estate's Network.IPAddress records (ADR-023); dhcp-scope/dns-zone are its config surfaces. Referenced by the dependency graph so shutdown/startup ordering accounts for hosts that need leases/resolution.

services.description: Which address capabilities this service provides. (IPAM-as-a-distinct-service dropped — addressing is modeled as Network.IPAddress records per ADR-023; there is no adopted IPAM vocabulary and its substance is covered by dhcp-scope + the IPAddress records.)

relationships.name: served_by
relationships.description: served_by — a host running this DHCP/DNS provider. The service stops before its host in a reverse-topo shutdown.

relationships.name: served_by
relationships.description: served_by — a VM running this DHCP/DNS provider. (One of BareMetalHost / VirtualMachine per instance.)

adopts.role: DHCP protocol concepts (lease/scope) — the neutral vocabulary; concrete servers (Kea/dnsmasq/...) are providers
